page: Microsoft Azure E96bds_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 672.0 GB of system memory and includes 3686 GB of local NVMe storage. It operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores all ranking in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is also a strong point, with uncached read speeds reaching 331.15 GB/sec and write speeds at 139.89 GB/sec. Cryptographic performance is particularly notable, with the Passmark CPU encryption test placing in the top 10% tier, aided by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA-NI instruction sets. However, single-core performance is moderate, with Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core scores falling in the middle 50% range.

The server exhibits distinct tradeoffs in workload execution. While it achieves strong results in database operations, Redis SET operations at 88.79 million operations per second, and static web serving, its performance on large-scale AI tasks is constrained. LLM inference for text generation on a large 70B model falls into the bottom 25% tier due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware. Consequently, this server is best suited for memory-intensive databases, enterprise applications, large-scale web serving, and parallelized CPU-bound workloads rather than deep learning or heavy single-threaded tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E96bds_v5 is an x86_64 server configured with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 672.0 GB of RAM and 3686 GB of local storage, running under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic execution, with Passmark encryption tests reaching the top 10% tier. Conversely, single-core performance remains in the average tier. The lack of dedicated GPU acceleration limits its utility for large language model text generation, where it performs in the bottom 25% for 70B models. However, its high memory capacity, fast memory bandwidth, and substantial local storage make it highly effective for memory-intensive database operations, Redis deployments, and parallelized enterprise workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96bds_v5 is a memory-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and 3.6 TB local storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96bds_v5 features 96 vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and 3.6 TB local storage, optimized for memory-intensive database and enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity memory and storage instance featuring ninety-six vCPUs, designed for demanding database operations and parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8370C at 2.8 GHz
672.0 GB of system memory and 3686 GB of bundled local storage
Strong multi-core, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic benchmark performance
Top 10% tier performance in Passmark CPU encryption tests
Underperforms in large-scale LLM text generation due to lack of GPU
Best suited for memory-intensive databases, Redis, and parallel enterprise applications
